meta.notes: For authorized testing only. Heuristic findings; validate manually.

Internal Server Error

The server encountered an internal error and was unable to complete your request. Either the server is overloaded or there is an error in the application.

findings.title: Null pointer dereference indication
findings.description: Null-like input '0' triggered error behavior suggesting improper null handling.
findings.recommendation: Add null checks before dereferencing objects; use safe navigation operators or default values.

findings.title: Uncaught exception (5xx response)
findings.description: Server returned a 5xx error to edge-case input, indicating unhandled exception or server error.

evidence.snippet:
Internal Server Error

The server encountered an internal error and was unable to complete your request. Either the server is overloaded or there is an error in the application.

findings.recommendation: Implement centralized exception handling and return generic error responses to clients while logging details server-side.

findings.cwe_name: Improper Check or Handling of Exceptional Conditions
findings.title: Significant response size anomaly
findings.description: Response size changed significantly from baseline (39B -> 265B). This indicates different code execution path or error handling for edge-case input.
findings.recommendation: Ensure consistent error handling across all input validation paths. Review why this input triggers different behavior.

findings.cwe_name: Server-generated Error Message Containing Sensitive Information
findings.title: Server error message containing sensitive information
findings.description: Response contains technical implementation details (SQL queries, database errors, paths, stack traces).

evidence.snippet:
Internal Server Error

The server encountered an internal error and was unable to complete your request. Either the server is overloaded or there is an error in the application.

findings.recommendation: Configure server to suppress technical details in error responses; use custom error handlers.

findings.cwe_name: Unexpected Status Code or Return Value
findings.title: Unexpected status code behavior
findings.description: Status code changed from 400 to 200 for edge-case input. This indicates inconsistent error handling or different execution paths.
findings.recommendation: Ensure consistent, safe error handling across code paths. Review why this input changes control flow.

findings.cwe_name: Improper Check for Unusual or Exceptional Conditions
findings.title: Improper check for unusual condition: Type confusion
findings.description: Application failed to properly handle unusual input condition (Type confusion), returning error status 400 instead of graceful validation.
findings.recommendation: Implement proper input validation for boundary conditions, size limits, and unusual inputs. Return 400 Bad Request with safe error messages instead of allowing server errors.

findings.title: Potential divide-by-zero vulnerability
findings.description: Zero input triggered error condition consistent with divide-by-zero or unsafe numeric handling.
findings.recommendation: Validate numeric inputs; explicitly guard division operations with zero checks and safe fallbacks.

findings.cwe_name: Improper Check for Unusual or Exceptional Conditions
findings.title: Improper check for unusual condition: Unhandled input condition
findings.description: Application failed to properly handle unusual input condition (Unhandled input condition), returning error status 500 instead of graceful validation.
findings.recommendation: Implement proper input validation for boundary conditions, size limits, and unusual inputs. Return 400 Bad Request with safe error messages instead of allowing server errors.

findings.title: Improper handling of exceptional input conditions
findings.description: Type confusion or special value input resulted in unusual status code 500, indicating inadequate exception handling.
findings.recommendation: Implement comprehensive input validation and exception handling for all data types and edge cases.

findings.cwe_name: Improper Check for Unusual or Exceptional Conditions
findings.title: Improper check for unusual condition: Encoding issue
findings.description: Application failed to properly handle unusual input condition (Encoding issue), returning error status 400 instead of graceful validation.
findings.recommendation: Implement proper input validation for boundary conditions, size limits, and unusual inputs. Return 400 Bad Request with safe error messages instead of allowing server errors.

findings.title: Missing parameter triggers error condition
findings.description: Omitting parameter 'divisor' caused an error response or significant behavior change. Required parameters should be validated gracefully.
findings.recommendation: Validate required parameters and return 400 Bad Request with a safe message instead of crashing or exposing errors.

findings.title: Default/generic error page detected
findings.description: Server returned default error page with technical details (status 500), rather than custom user-friendly page.

evidence.snippet:
Internal Server Error

The server encountered an internal error and was unable to complete your request. Either the server is overloaded or there is an error in the application.

findings.recommendation: Implement custom error pages for all error conditions; suppress technical details from default server pages.

findings.cwe_name: Unexpected Status Code or Return Value
findings.title: Unexpected status code behavior
findings.description: Status code changed from 400 to 200 for edge-case input. This indicates inconsistent error handling or different execution paths.
findings.recommendation: Ensure consistent, safe error handling across code paths. Review why this input changes control flow.

findings.title: Extra parameter triggers error condition
findings.description: Adding unexpected parameter caused error response or behavior change. Applications should safely ignore or reject unexpected inputs.
findings.recommendation: Whitelist expected parameters and reject/ignore extras; log suspicious requests for monitoring.

findings.cwe_name: Improper Handling of Insufficient Permissions or Privileges
findings.title: Improper permission parameter handling
findings.description: Privilege-related extra parameter caused behavioral change, suggesting improper permission handling.
findings.recommendation: Validate and sanitize all parameters; ignore unexpected permission/privilege parameters.

evidence.snippet:
Internal Server Error

The server encountered an internal error and was unable to complete your request. Either the server is overloaded or there is an error in the application.

findings.recommendation: Implement custom error pages for all error conditions; suppress technical details from default server pages.

findings.title: Potential failing-open behavior
findings.description: Baseline indicates access denied (403) but edge-case request returned 200. This suggests inconsistent authorization checks.
findings.recommendation: Review authorization logic to ensure it fails closed on all exceptional conditions; test for parameter pollution.

findings.title: Insufficient privilege handling
findings.description: Adding privilege-related parameter bypassed authorization (baseline 403 -> 200).
findings.recommendation: Implement proper authorization checks; whitelist parameters and reject unexpected privilege escalation attempts.

findings.description: Status code changed from 403 to 200 for edge-case input. This indicates inconsistent error handling or different execution paths.
findings.recommendation: Ensure consistent, safe error handling across code paths. Review why this input changes control flow.

Internal Server Error

findings.recommendation: Configure server to suppress technical details in error responses; use custom error handlers.

findings.cwe_name: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information
findings.title: Error message contains sensitive information
findings.description: Response contains technical error details (stack traces, database errors, file paths, etc.) that may aid attackers in reconnaissance.
findings.recommendation: Disable debug mode in production. Use generic error pages for clients and log detailed errors server-side only.

findings.cwe_name: Improper Check or Handling of Exceptional Conditions
findings.title: Significant response size anomaly
findings.description: Response size changed significantly from baseline (51B -> 72B). This indicates different code execution path or error handling for edge-case input.
findings.recommendation: Ensure consistent error handling across all input validation paths. Review why this input triggers different behavior.

findings.cwe_name: Improper Check for Unusual or Exceptional Conditions
findings.title: Improper check for unusual condition: Unhandled input condition
findings.description: Application failed to properly handle unusual input condition (Unhandled input condition), returning error status 500 instead of graceful validation.
findings.recommendation: Implement proper input validation for boundary conditions, size limits, and unusual inputs. Return 400 Bad Request with safe error messages instead of allowing server errors.

findings.title: Improper handling of exceptional input conditions
findings.description: Type confusion or special value input resulted in unusual status code 500, indicating inadequate exception handling.
findings.recommendation: Implement comprehensive input validation and exception handling for all data types and edge cases.

findings.title: Potential unchecked return value
findings.description: Edge-case input caused subtle response changes without status code change, suggesting error condition not properly checked/propagated.
findings.recommendation: Check return values from all operations; propagate errors appropriately rather than silently failing.

findings.title: Default/generic error page detected
findings.description: Server returned default error page with technical details (status 500), rather than custom user-friendly page.

evidence.snippet:
Internal Server Error

The server encountered an internal error and was unable to complete your request. Either the server is overloaded or there is an error in the application.

findings.recommendation: Implement custom error pages for all error conditions; suppress technical details from default server pages.
